title: AI and the Future of Cyber Careers
modules: Module 1: What AI Is Actually Automating in Security Operations Today
Module 2: The New Threat Surface — Jobs Created by AI-Driven Attacks
Module 3: Skills That Remain Irreplaceable — Judgment, Adversarial Thinking, and Context
Module 4: Emerging Roles — AI Red Teamer, ML Security Engineer, Prompt Auditor
Module 5: Reskilling Roadmap — Where to Invest Time and Credentials Now
Module 6: How Organizations Are Restructuring Security Teams Around AI Tools
Module 7: Building a Career Strategy That Holds Up as the Field Keeps Shifting
synopsis: AI is reshaping which cybersecurity tasks get automated, which roles get eliminated, and which new specializations are urgently needed — but most career guidance hasn't caught up with what's actually happening inside security operations centers. This course gives both working professionals and curious non-specialists an honest, evidence-based picture of where the field is heading and what to do about it.
rationale: Widespread anxiety about AI replacing security jobs is driving poor career decisions, while the real story — significant role transformation rather than wholesale elimination — remains underexplained.
learning_outcome: Learners can identify which parts of their current or target security role are most exposed to automation and build a concrete, prioritized plan for skill development that reflects the AI-augmented security landscape.
source_signals: Will AI Replace Cyber Security Jobs? | Is AI Going To Replace Cybersecurity ?
Comparing AI agents to cybersecurity professionals in real-world pen testing
